summary: Recent updates to WebRender, the part of Firefox responsible for graphical rendering, have introduced several enhancements aimed at improving performance and visual fidelity. Notably, support for **CSS corner-shape**, a feature that allows web designers to create rounded and custom-shaped corners on elements, has been added to enhance aesthetics in web pages. Additionally, improvements in content snapping and performance optimizations, like the removal of redundant data and more efficient handling of textures, contribute to a smoother user experience and better resource management. These changes collectively ensure that Firefox keeps up with modern web standards and provides users with a visually appealing and responsive browsing experience.
